purpose: Define the audio-first subtitle alignment protocol for minimal psychology explainer videos.

standard_flow.description: Split narration into stable sentence-level voiceover_units. Each unit must include id, scene_id, text, target_duration_sec, intended_pause_after_sec, emphasis_words, and timing_status.
Generate one final voiceover audio file from voiceover_units. ElevenLabs is the default provider.
Read real audio duration using media parser or ffprobe-compatible tooling.
Rebuild subtitle_timeline.horizontal.json from the real audio duration or forced-alignment timestamps. Estimated timing is only allowed for rough previews.
Set composition duration to cover the final audio duration. Add only a small tail buffer unless the video intentionally needs a silent outro.
Render SubtitleTrack from the rebuilt subtitle timeline instead of old hand-estimated frames.

timing_rules.subtitle_safe_area: bottom area below psych divider line is reserved for global subtitles only

fallback_alignment_methods.description: Use word or sentence timestamps from transcription/alignment when available.
If forced alignment is unavailable, distribute timing by voiceover unit text length and target duration, then scale to real audio duration.
For production, inspect preview and manually adjust cues with obvious early/late subtitle issues.

hard_rules: Do not render a final voiceover video using only hand-estimated subtitle frames.
If voiceover text changes, subtitle_timeline.horizontal.json must be regenerated.
If TTS voice, speed, or model changes, subtitle_timeline.horizontal.json must be regenerated.
Scene-local text must not overlap the bottom global subtitle area.
Transparent subtitle style is preferred for this template; do not reintroduce a black subtitle box unless explicitly requested.
